content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 16, 1990

Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
605 E. Hazelwood Dr.
Lemoore, CA  93245

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-269

Dear Ms. Wyckoff:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1  We apologize for the delay in
responding to your request.

QUESTION

     Were you required to file a Statement of Organization (Form
410) and obtain an identification number when you formed the
Committee to Reelect Wyckoff in the Fall of 1988?

CONCLUSION

     Candidates were not required to file a Form 410 prior to
January 1, 1989, even if they raised or spent $1,000 in a calendar
year for their election to office.

FACTS

     In the Fall of 1988 you announced your candidacy for
reelection to the Lemoore City Council.  At that time, you filed a
Form 410 and applied to the Secretary of State's office for a
committee identification number.  Since you did not receive notice
from the Secretary of State's office of receipt of your
application, you called their office in March of 1989.  The
Secretary of State's office indicated to you at that time that
they had no record of receiving your application.


1          Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Barbara Wyckoff
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Prior to January 1, 1989, a candidate was not required to
file a Statement of Organization (Form 410), even if the candidate
received contributions totaling $1,000 in a calendar year.  Such a
candidate was required to file campaign disclosure statements
under the Act (Section 84100-84309) whether or not he or she had
formed a committee.  (Multiholland Advice Letter, No. F-86-108, copy
enclosed.)  Due to passage of Proposition 73², which became
effective January 1, 1989, candidates are required to file Form
410 if they raise or spend $1,000 in a calendar year for campaign-
related expenses.  (1990 Information Manual A, Page 27.)

Since all candidates who receive $1,000 or more in
contributions in a calendar year are now required to file Form
410, if you received $1,000 or more in 1989 or 1990, you were
required to file the Statement of Organization (Form 410) within
10 days.  A Form 410 is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have any additional questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

___________________________

2    On September 25, 1990, the United States District Court,
Eastern District of California, in the case of Service Employees
International Union, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission
(Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM), ruled that portions of
Proposition 73 were unconstitutional.  However, the portions of
Proposition 73 discussed in this letter were unaffected by this
decision.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 23, 1990

Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
605 E. Hazelwood Drive
Lemoore, CA 93245

Dear Ms. Wyckoff:

According to our records, it appears that you may not have
received a response to you letter of March 31, 1989. Please find
enclosed a copy of your letter and Jeanne Pritchard's letter of
May 5, 1989.

At your convenience, please call me at (916) 322-5662 to confirm
whether you received our response.

I apologize for any inconvenience caused and look forward to
hearing from you.

Sincerely,

Hilda P. Perez
Technical Assistance
& Analysis Division

Enclosures

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

89-269

FPPC
May 3 2:13 PM '09

May 1, 1989


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

     Enclosed is a copy of a letter I sent you on March 31, 1989. I have had no reply to date, and wanted to again ask for a written opinion regarding the question in my previous letter.

     Thanking you for your prompt attention to this matter, I am,

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
                                        605 E. Hazelwood Drive
                                        Lemoore, CA 93245

March 31, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

     In the Fall of 1988 I announced my candidacy for reelec-
tion to the Lemoore City Council.

     I understood at that time that I needed to form a campaign
committee, and apply to the Secretary of State for a number
for the committee.  So, I did.  A copy of said application is
enclosed.

     I never received notification from the Secretary of State
as to what my committee's number was, and so I recently called
them.  They have no record of my application; apparently it
was lost in the mail.

     All of my campaign statements were filed under the name of
my committee, and I always noted that the ID# had been applied
for.

     On Thursday, March 30, 1988, I called FPPC and spoke to a
"Kevin" about this situation.  He stated that it didn't matter
that there was no record of the formation of my committee;
that the requirement I was operating under didn't go into
effect until 1/1/89.  I asked if I could have this statement
in writing, and he said yes if I made a written request
therefor.

     Would you please send me written confirmation that I have
not done anything wrong in failing to obtain an identification
number for the Committee to Reelect Wyckoff.

     If you need additional information regarding this matter,
please advise.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
                                        605 E. Hazelwood Drive
                                        Lemoore, CA 93245

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 5, 1989


Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
605 E. Hazelwood Drive
Lemoore, CA 93245

Re: Letter No. 89-269

Dear Ms. Wyckoff:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 3, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

JP:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

March 31, 1989


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

In the Fall of 1988 I announced my candidacy for membership to the Desert (D'Amour).

I was advised by a Staff member from a local campaign consulting firm that I would need to file a Form 700 (Statement of Economic Interests).

On Thursday, March 23, 1989, I called FPPC and spoke to a woman who provided about this situation. I stated that it didn't matter if that didn't matter what I needed to do to become part of the Committee to Reject Wyckoff, since the Committee was organized under district 90 into effect until 11/30/88. I asked if I could have this statement in writing, and he said yes if I made a written request therefor.

Would you please send me written confirmation that I have not done anything wrong in failing to obtain an identification number for the Committee to Reject Wyckoff.

If you need additional information regarding this matter, please advise.

Very truly yours,


Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
605 E. Hazelwood Drive
Lemoore, CA 93245

May 1, 1989


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

Enclosed is a copy of a letter I sent you on March 31,
1989. I have not yet received any reply and wanted to confirm that
for a written opinion regarding the question in my previous letter.

I would appreciate it if you could provide me with a reply soon.

Sincerely,


[illegible]

[illegible], CA [illegible]

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 5, 1989


Barbara Clawson Wyckoff
605 E Hazelwood Drive
Lemoore, CA 93245

Re: Letter No. 89-295

Dear Ms. Wyckoff:

[illegible text in middle section]

are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

410) and obtain an identification number when you formed the 
Committee to Reelect Wyckoff in the Fall of 1988?

CONCLUSION: Candidates were not required to file a Form 410 prior to 
January 1, 1989, even if they raised or spent $1,000 in a calendar 
year for their election to office.
